what_it_does: This file is a small Node.js ESM launcher that re-executes the real CLI entry point at episodic-memory.js by spawning the current Node interpreter with inherited stdio and forwarding all command-line arguments.
observations: Uses Node spawn from child_process to run another script.
Resolves __dirname via fileURLToPath and dirname for ESM compatibility.
Uses stdio: 'inherit' so the child controls the terminal.
Exits with the child's exit code, defaulting to 0 if null.
standouts: Thin wrapper that does no parsing or logic itself.
conventions: Exit-code propagation to the parent process.
notes: A pass-through launcher whose only job is to start the sibling episodic-memory.js script under the current Node runtime.
questions: Why is this wrapper needed instead of invoking episodic-memory.js directly?
purpose_vs_actual: It appears to be a launcher wrapper and it does exactly that; stated purpose cannot be verified from this file alone.
intention_signals: Provide a stable CLI entry point that delegates execution to an adjacent implementation file while preserving arguments, stdio, and exit status.
contribution: Provides a thin, argument-preserving execution shim that a fused system could keep as a CLI entry point if the surrounding tooling expects this exact file path.
summary_for_embedding: A Node.js ESM wrapper that spawns the sibling episodic-memory.js script with inherited stdio and forwarded arguments.
intention_for_embedding: To offer a stable, executable CLI facade that delegates to the actual implementation file.
